revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:е…өдё»зҘһзӨҫ (дё№жіўеёӮ)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Hyozu Shrine | location={{ill|HyЕҚgo Prefecture|en|HyЕҚgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=HyЕҚgo Prefecture|1=HyЕҚgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Tamba, HyЕҚgo|en|Tamba, HyЕҚgo|qid=Q877196|lt=Tamba City|1=Tamba, HyЕҚgo}} Kasuga Town, Kuroi 2967 | coordinates={{Coord|35|10|39.4|N|135|5|46.2|E|scale:20000_region:jp-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|en|ЕҢkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=ЕҢnamuchi no Kami|1=ЕҢkuninushi}} (ЕҢnamochi no ЕҢkami)
{{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|е°‘еҪҰеҗҚе‘Ҫ|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Sukunahikona no ЕҢkami)
{{ill|Amatsukayama no Mikoto|ja|еӨ©йҰҷеұұе‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukayama no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (Amatsukayama no ЕҢkami)
{{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|qid=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} (Ebisu no ЕҢkami) * {{ill|Chiyou|lt=Hyozu-no-Kami|ja|е…өдё»зҘһ|en|Chiyou|ru|Р§Рё Р®|fr|Chiyou|es|Chi You|zh|иҡ©е°Ө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | consecration_year={{ill|TenpyЕҚ|en|TenpyЕҚ|qid=Q1199879|lt=TenpyЕҚ|1=TenpyЕҚ}} 18 (746 CE) | Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine (Shinto)|ja|зңҢзӨҫ|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | festival={{ill|October 9|en|October 9|qid=Q2923|lt=October 9|1=October 9}} | Architecture={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Hyozu-jinja tamba02s3200.jpg|300px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | Map={{location map|Japan HyЕҚgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=10|lat_sec=39.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=5|lon_sec=46.2|label=Hyozu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Hyozu-jinja tamba04s3200.jpg|thumb|View behind the main hall]] '''Hyozu Shrine''' (е…өдё»зҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tamba, HyЕҚgo|en|Tamba, HyЕҚgo|qid=Q877196|lt=Tamba City|1=Tamba, HyЕҚgo}}, {{ill|HyЕҚgo Prefecture|en|HyЕҚgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=HyЕҚgo Prefecture|1=HyЕҚgo Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. == Overview == Hyozu Shrine is one of the 19 shrines across Japan dedicated to the guardian deity of warriors, {{ill|HyЕҚzu Shrine|ja|е…өдё»зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HyЕҚzu Shrine|qq=}}. It is a shrine of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tamba Province|1=Tanba Province}}, founded in {{ill|TenpyЕҚ|en|TenpyЕҚ|qid=Q1199879|lt=TenpyЕҚ|1=TenpyЕҚ}} 18 (746 CE). The enshrined deities include ЕҢnamochi no ЕҢkami, Sukunahikona no ЕҢkami, Amatsukayama no ЕҢkami, and Ebisu no ЕҢkami. The shrine is widely revered as a protector of life and property. Since the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it has also been known as a guardian against smallpox. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are considered a "sacred forest" ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|en|Chinju no Mori|qid=Q11651158|lt=Chinju no Mori|1=Chinju no Mori}}). Prominent trees include a {{ill|Magnolia compressa|en|Magnolia compressa|qid=Q11291825|lt=magnolia obovata|1=Magnolia compressa}} (34 meters tall) behind the shrine hall, along with ancient {{ill|Aphananthe aspera|en|Aphananthe aspera|qid=Q4779482|lt=zelkova|1=Aphananthe aspera}} and cedar trees. The area also features a cluster of {{ill|Japanese chinquapin|ja|гӮігӮёгӮӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese chinquapin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғ„гғ–гғ©гӮёгӮӨ|1=Castanopsis cuspidata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2717620}} trees. These natural elements are designated as an Environmental Green Conservation Area and a Natural Monument by Tamba City. The torii bears the inscription "е…өдё»зӨҫ," written and donated by the Inner Minister {{ill|Konoe Motosaki|en|Konoe Motosaki|qid=Q3198720|lt=Konoe Motohisa|1=Konoe Motosaki}} in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 11. The shrine also preserves many offerings from the {{ill|Konoe family|en|Konoe family|qid=Q1781840|lt=Konoe family|1=Konoe family}}. == location and Access == * location: 2967 Kasuga Town, Kuroi, Tamba City, HyЕҚgo Prefecture * Access: 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kuroi Station (HyЕҚgo)|ja|й»’дә•й§…|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroi Station (HyЕҚgo)|1=й»’дә•й§…|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q901372}} on the {{ill|Fukuchiyama Line|en|Fukuchiyama Line|qid=Q1154538|lt=JR Fukuchiyama Line|1=Fukuchiyama Line}} == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Kuroi Castle|en|Kuroi Castle|qid=Q11677999|lt=Kuroi Castle|1=Kuroi Castle}} * {{ill|KЕҚzen-ji (Tamba City)|ja|иҲҲзҰ…еҜә (дё№жіўеёӮ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚzen-ji (Tamba City)|1=Kozen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613439}} * {{ill|Tamba Kasuga Local History Museum|ja|дё№жіўеёӮз«ӢжҳҘж—Ҙйғ·еңҹиіҮж–ҷйӨЁ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamba Kasuga Local History Museum|1=Tamba Municipal Kasuga Local Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513712}} * {{ill|Tamba Kasuga History and Folklore Museum|ja|дё№жіўеёӮз«ӢжҳҘж—ҘжӯҙеҸІж°‘дҝ—иіҮж–ҷйӨЁ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamba Kasuga History and Folklore Museum|1=Kasuga Folk Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513640}} * {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Tamba Obachan no Sato|ja|йҒ“гҒ®й§…дё№жіўгҒҠгҒ°гҒӮгҒЎгӮғгӮ“гҒ®йҮҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Tamba Obachan no Sato|1=Roadside station Tanba Obaachan-no-sato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641123}} * {{ill|HyЕҚgo Prefectural Hikami High School|ja|е…өеә«зңҢз«Ӣж°·дёҠй«ҳзӯүеӯҰж Ў|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HyЕҚgo Prefectural Hikami High School|1=Hikami High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393045}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tourism in HyЕҚgo Prefecture|ja|е…өеә«зңҢгҒ®иҰіе…үең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tourism in HyЕҚgo Prefecture|1=Visitor attractions in Hyogo prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392864}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Hyozu-jinja, Tamba}} * [http://www9.plala.or.jp/hyozu-jinja/yuisyoannai.html Official website of Hyozu Shrine in Tamba City, HyЕҚgo Prefecture] (Retrieved September 2, 2016) * [https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/spot/index.php?act=detail&id=200672 Hyozu Shrine on HyЕҚgo Tourism Guide] (Retrieved September 2, 2016) * [http://kitakinki.gr.jp/spot/528 Hyozu Shrine on Kitakinki Tourism] (Retrieved September 2, 2016) * [http://www.tambacity-kankou.jp/members/detail.php?id=789 Hyozu Shrine on Tamba City Tourism Association] (Retrieved September 2, 2016) * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/hyogo/detail/28644ag2130012107 Hyozu Shrine on Nationwide Sightseeing Guide (Japan Tourism Association)] (Retrieved September 2, 2016) * [http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/rentoku/syaji/201407/0007130996.shtml Kobe Newspaper NEXT | Shrines and Temples | Hyozu Shrine (Tamba City)] (Retrieved September 2, 2016) {{Shinto Horizontal}}
